mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series 520i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 520i engine typically costs £2,500–£5,500 supply only, with the B48 and N20 variants at the higher end. Fitting adds £800–£1,300 depending on the specialist. Used engines can be found from £800–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your 5 Series.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series 520i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you have. The M10, M20, M50 and M54 are fundamentally robust — with cooling system maintenance critical on the M54 and timing belt changes essential on the M20. The N43 (E60) and N20 (F10) are the riskier modern engines — timing chain guide failure is the defining issue. The B48 (G30, 2017+) is the most refined and reliable, with timing chain issues resolved.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series 520i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20 timing chain guide failure (F10) — plastic guides become brittle and can destroy the engine. N43 timing chain guide and injector issues (E60). M54 cooling system failures (E39) — plastic water pump and thermostat housing components fail. M20 timing belt requires regular replacement. The B48 (G30) is significantly improved.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 520i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the generation and vehicle condition. Against a well-kept G30 worth £15,000–£25,000, a £4,000–£5,500 recon is 15–35% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired E39 worth £2,000–£4,000, the same recon may exceed our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. F10 values (£5,000–£12,000) make replacement borderline.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 5 Series 520i engine code?
Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The chain guides must be replaced — they are the weak point. A preventative replacement of the timing chain and guides costs £1,500–£3,000. A snapped chain almost always destroys the engine, requiring a £3,500–£5,500+ replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The 520i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Original 520i — 2.0L M10 four-cylinder with Kugelfischer mechanical injection; 130 bhp
SOHC straight-six 2.0L; 125 bhp; smooth and reliable; timing belt must be changed regularly
129 bhp; same M20 six-cylinder; replaced by M50 in 1990
DOHC 24-valve 2.0L six; 150 bhp; VANOS from 1992; robust and refined
2.2L DOHC six; 170 bhp; double-VANOS; cooling system is the critical weakness
2.0L N/A four-cylinder; 170 bhp; direct injection; timing chain guide and injector issues
2.0L turbo four; 184 bhp; timing chain guide failure is the critical weakness
Modern 2.0L turbo four; 184 bhp; robust and efficient; timing chain issues resolved

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect current 520i replacement costs. The B48 and N20 variants typically sit at the higher end of the range. See the Era Map above for generation-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 520i engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 520i? Check These First
buyIf: * G30 (2017+) with the B48 engine (most reliable modern 520i)
* F10 with documented timing chain guide replacement (pre-2015 critical)
* E39 with cooling system recently overhauled
* E34 with M50 engine (post-1990) — robust DOHC design
* Full service history with regular oil changes (all generations)
avoidIf: * F10 N20 with cold-start rattle and no chain history — assume it's overdue
* E60 N43 with cold-start rattle or rough running — timing chain and injector issues likely
* E39 with overheating history or unexplained coolant loss — cooling system may be compromised
* No service history — all 520i engines are sensitive to oil quality
* M20-engined cars (E28/E34) with no timing belt history — assume it's overdue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 520i is one of BMW's most enduring badges — spanning seven generations from 1972 to the present. The engine has evolved from the M10 four-cylinder through the M20 and M50 six-cylinders to the modern turbocharged N20 and B48 four-cylinders. The M10, M20, M50 and M54 are fundamentally robust — with cooling system maintenance critical on the M54. The N20 and N43 are the riskier modern engines — timing chain guide failure is the defining issue. The B48 (G30) is the most refined and reliable. With typical values ranging from £3,000 (E39) to £25,000+ (G30), an engine replacement is a significant investment — the B48-engined G30 is the safest choice.

cta.label: → Compare 520i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 5 Series 520i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling (N20/N43 timing chain warning) — see the map above
* Service History — Including which engine era and oil-change discipline
* Timing Chain/Belt — For N20/N43: check for chain work; for M20: timing belt history critical
* Cooling System — Check for coolant leaks, recent water pump/thermostat replacement (M54 critical)
* VANOS Operation — Test low-end torque; any flat spot may indicate VANOS seal wear (M50/M54)
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes read; unresolved issues may indicate timing, injector or sensor problems
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
